title: My Daily Newsletter Digest — Monday, June 22, 2026
description: This episode covers the escalating standoff between Anthropic and the U.S. government over export controls on its top models, including a "Free Fable" letter from security researchers and tensions at the G7 AI summit. It also tracks Google DeepMind's loss of star researchers Noam Shazeer and John Jumper, dueling studies on whether AI augments or eliminates jobs, Snap's new true-AR Specs, and Midjourney's surprise full-body medical scanner and spa plans. Other stories include SpaceX's sixty-billion-dollar acquisition of Cursor, enterprise "token panic" over AI costs, Databricks' open-systems strategy, agent governance, China's rising AI standing, and shifting public sentiment toward the technology.

title: My Daily Newsletter Digest — Wednesday, June 24, 2026
description: Today's episode centers on AI entering the workplace, leading with Anthropic's Claude Tag, which embeds Claude into Slack as a delegatable "team member," and a Confidential Computing Summit push for data decentralization and agent security. It also covers how AI is reshaping hiring and the skills employers now want, Meta's cheaper $299 smart glasses, Stanford's new Proto framework for combining AI biology models, and a rapid-fire roundup including Nvidia's BioNeMo toolkit, new OCR and voice models, and government scrutiny of Meta's AI.

title: My Daily Newsletter Digest — Thursday, June 25, 2026
description: OpenAI launches its first custom inference chip, Jalapeño, built with Broadcom, as the company pushes to own every layer of the AI stack. The episode also covers KPMG data on how workers are adopting AI agents, Figma's new "team sport" AI tools from its Config event, a $500 million nonprofit called Intercept aiming to end the common cold, and the latest developments in Anthropic's regulatory standoff with the government. Plus quick hits on AI talent moves, Google DeepMind's Gemini computer-use feature, a World Cup ticket-scouting tip, and a reader's Claude-built app for selective mutism.
